signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.44 — Middle East hostilities and a +5.24% surge in WTI oil prices drive USD safe-haven demand, overriding traditional JPY haven flows. 25d RR at -1.58 (Saxo) confirms institutional demand for downside protection despite the spot rally, signaling extreme intervention anxiety as price holds above the 160.00 threshold. Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 1.0% (+275bp) carry remains the structural floor, but geopolitical volatility introduces sharp reversal risk. Carry dominates only while VIX remains in 'normal' regime (16.8).

signals.title: USD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme + Volatility Premium
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.8138 — G10 scorecard identifies a significant bilateral divergence as USD outperforms (+0.286%) and CHF lags (-0.666%). The 375bp Fed-SNB carry advantage is amplified by a massive +16.6% Vol Risk Premium (IV 22.8% vs HV30 6.2%), indicating the options market is pricing in extreme tail risk or a rapid regime shift. USD CESI (+63) vs CHF (0) supports the fundamental divergence. Watch for a break of the 1M high to confirm momentum extension.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Oil Correlation Break + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4151 — The pair's correlation with WTI oil has collapsed to -0.899 (z = -2.02σ), as CAD fails to capture the +5.24% oil rally due to broader USD strength and Middle East risk-off. Retail sentiment is 80% short (Myfxbook), diverging from the COT LF net short of -87,846 (specs also short). This rare alignment of retail and institutional shorts against a rising price trend suggests a high-conviction short-squeeze risk if Governor Macklem maintains a neutral stance at the upcoming MPR.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Data Underperformance + Volatility Hedging
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1391 — Persistent data misses (CESI -11) and a restrictive real rate of only +0.03% weigh on the Euro. While the nominal Fed-ECB spread is 150bp, the options market is pricing an elevated +2.3% VRP (IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.8%), signaling that traders are paying up for protection against further downside. COT LF net short -66,057 aligns with the bearish fundamental bias. Sustained trading below 1.1400 targets the 1M low.
